name: BASMAH
mission: When you brighten a life, you ignite change that extends far beyond one person. Each individual touched goes on to inspire and uplift those around them, creating a ripple effect of hope and transformation. At BASMAH, we are committed to brightening as many lives as possible—because every life changed brings the world one step closer to a brighter future.
theoryOfChange: The organization states it focuses on sustainable development to empower communities, emphasizing that short-term help is needed but long-term stability is the goal, and aims to enable people to help themselves and their communities.
scoreSummary: Bangladesh American Society of Muslim Aid for Humanity Inc shows exceptional alignment and moderate impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).
zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://basmah-bd.org/zakat (Source: https://basmah-bd.org/zakat)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: Claims 5 geographic areas but no foreign grants on Schedule F
keyConcerns.detail: Organization lists international operations but IRS filings show no foreign grant activity.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate overall score due to its high alignment with humanitarian needs and donor values, though its impact score is tempered by a lack of external cost-effectiveness data.

dimension_explanations.alignment: With an alignment score of 43/50, the charity demonstrates a high fit for Muslim donors by offering zakat-eligible programs that address urgent humanitarian crises [2][4]. Its mission to move beyond short-term help toward long-term stability resonates with core Islamic charitable principles [2].
dimension_explanations.impact: BASMAH operates direct service programs including medical centers and orphanages, but its impact score of 22/50 reflects a need for more transparent data regarding cost per beneficiary [1][3]. The organization maintains a low working capital of 0.8 months, which may affect its ability to sustain long-term programs during funding fluctuations [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Bangladesh American Society of Muslim Aid for Humanity Inc (BASMAH) provides critical humanitarian relief and long-term developmental support to vulnerable populations, including Rohingya refugees.
strengths: Direct service provider for high-urgency humanitarian needs in the Rohingya refugee crisis
Clear zakat policy and eligibility for Muslim donors
Diverse program portfolio covering healthcare, education, and orphan care
baseline_narrative.summary: BASMAH focuses on empowering communities through sustainable aid, operating medical centers, educational facilities, and orphan centers to foster long-term stability [1][3]. The organization is zakat-eligible, allowing donors to fulfill their religious obligations through various asnaf categories [2][4].

all_citations.claim: empowering people and enabling them to help themselves
all_citations.quote: Our Vision is to empower people and enable them to help themselves and their communities.
Your donation can help provide food, water, and medical care.

confidence.notes: Strong alignment data; financial health metrics indicate lean operations.

alignment.explanation: The organization is a <cite id="2">verified zakat-eligible entity</cite> serving <cite id="2">underserved populations</cite> in Muslim-majority regions.
alignment.improvement: Alignment is already high, but could be furthered by <cite id="1">expanding services to additional underserved geographies</cite> with limited nonprofit coverage.

credibility.explanation: BASMAH demonstrates high transparency through its <cite id="1">Candid Platinum Seal</cite> and has been recognized as a <cite id="1">Top-Rated Non-Profit in 2024 and 2025</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the organization should <cite id="3">expand its board size</cite> beyond the current three members to enhance independent oversight.

impact.explanation: The charity utilizes a <cite id="1">direct service delivery model</cite> and has a <cite id="1">documented theory of change</cite> focused on long-term stability.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="1">publishing consistent beneficiary counts</cite> to allow for rigorous cost-effectiveness analysis.

rich_narrative.headline: The Bangladesh American Society of Muslim Aid for Humanity Inc (BASMAH) provides critical humanitarian relief and long-term developmental support to vulnerable populations, including Rohingya refugees.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support direct humanitarian aid for Rohingya refugees through a zakat-eligible, high-growth organization.
donor_motivations: Want to fulfill zakat across all eight asnaf categories
Prioritize direct service delivery over advocacy
Focus on the Rohingya refugee crisis in Bangladesh
Value high transparency seals from third-party evaluators
giving_considerations: The organization is relatively young, founded in 2018
Lean reserves mean donations are likely deployed immediately
Governance is currently managed by a small board
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking organizations with large financial endowments or those requiring multi-year longitudinal impact studies.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects <cite id="1">verified outcomes</cite> and a strong theory of change, though cost-per-beneficiary data is currently missing.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: BASMAH aims to empower individuals through <cite id="1">better opportunities</cite> so they can <cite id="1">improve their own lives and communities</cite>.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that humanitarian aid effectively transitions from short-term relief to long-term stability.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: The <cite id="3">42.1% revenue growth</cite> and expansion into multiple countries indicate a high capacity to absorb new funding.

strengths.detail: BASMAH provides <cite id="1">direct service delivery</cite> for high-urgency needs within the <cite id="1">Rohingya refugee camps</cite>.
The organization maintains a <cite id="2">clear zakat policy</cite> covering <cite id="2">eight distinct asnaf categories</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: BASMAH's operational strength lies in its <cite id="1">direct service model</cite>, which ensures that aid reaches beneficiaries without excessive intermediary layers. Their commitment to transparency is evidenced by their <cite id="1">Platinum Seal from Candid</cite>, the highest level of recognition for nonprofit disclosure. Furthermore, their rapid financial expansion, with <cite id="3">revenue growing to over $4.1 million</cite>, demonstrates strong donor trust and a <cite id="3">42.1% growth rate</cite> that allows for the scaling of <cite id="1">medical centers and educational facilities</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: founded in 2018, the Bangladesh American Society of Muslim Aid for Humanity Inc (BASMAH) is a humanitarian organization dedicated to <cite id="1">empowering people and enabling them to help themselves</cite>. The organization has experienced rapid growth, reporting <cite id="3">total revenue of $4,195,860 in 2023</cite>, representing a <cite id="3">3-year revenue CAGR of 42.1%</cite>. BASMAH operates primarily in Bangladesh, with a strategic focus on the <cite id="1">Rohingya crisis response</cite>, providing essential services to one of the world's most vulnerable refugee populations. Their program portfolio is diverse, spanning <cite id="1">education, healthcare, and orphan care</cite>, alongside critical <cite id="1">water, sanitation, and hygiene (WASH) initiatives</cite>. As a <cite id="2">zakat-eligible charity</cite>, BASMAH provides a transparent mechanism for Muslim donors to fulfill their religious obligations, specifically targeting <cite id="2">eight asnaf categories</cite> including the poor (fuqara) and needy (masakin). While the organization maintains a <cite id="1">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, its financial structure shows a <cite id="3">working capital of 0.8 months</cite>, suggesting a lean operational model that prioritizes immediate aid delivery over large cash reserves.
